# scroll-zoom-thing

> CSS 3D perspective parallax for MkDocs Material — no JS, pure CSS. A reusable template for building documentation/marketing sites with a layered AVIF parallax hero.

## Start here

If you are an LLM/agent driving this repo, the **canonical operator's manual** is [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md). It has 28 sections covering: orientation, mental model, file classification, decision tree, nine workflows, verification, common mistakes, depth math, browser quirks, performance, accessibility, security, glossary.

After `AGENTS.md`, the most useful files in priority order:

- [`templates/README.md`](templates/README.md) — scaffolding a new site from a starter template
- [`skills/setup-parallax/SKILL.md`](skills/setup-parallax/SKILL.md) — wiring the parallax from scratch
- [`skills/tune-layers/SKILL.md`](skills/tune-layers/SKILL.md) — diagnosing visual problems
- [`skills/convert-images/SKILL.md`](skills/convert-images/SKILL.md) — PNG→AVIF pipeline
- [`skills/generate-prompts/SKILL.md`](skills/generate-prompts/SKILL.md) — AI prompts for hero artwork
- [`agents/parallax-agent.md`](agents/parallax-agent.md) — parallax-focused persona
- [`agents/image-agent.md`](agents/image-agent.md) — imagery-focused persona

## Source files (the four that matter)

- [`mkdocs.yml`](mkdocs.yml) — site config
- [`overrides/home.html`](overrides/home.html) — hero template (Jinja)
- [`src/assets/stylesheets/home.css`](src/assets/stylesheets/home.css) — parallax engine
- [`src/assets/hero/`](src/assets/hero/) — AVIF layers

## Mental model

`perspective` on the scroll container `.mdx-parallax` establishes a 3D context. Each `<picture>` layer uses `translateZ(-N)` to sit at a different depth. The browser's natural 3D projection makes deeper layers move slower during scroll. `scale(depth + 1)` compensates for the depth-induced size reduction.

There is **no JavaScript** in the parallax. Two tiny browser-quirk scripts handle Safari and Firefox first-paint issues; that's it.

## Per-layer variables

- `--md-parallax-depth` — back-distance. Higher = slower scroll. Default ladder: `8, 5, 2, 1` (back to front).
- `--md-image-position` — `object-position` X percent. Tunes which slice of the image is visible.

## The ten variables you customize per project

1. `site_name` (mkdocs.yml)
2. `site_description` (mkdocs.yml)
3. `site_url` (mkdocs.yml)
4. `repo_url` / `repo_name` / `edit_uri` (mkdocs.yml)
5. Hero `<h1>` (overrides/home.html)
6. Hero `<p>` (overrides/home.html)
7. CTA labels and hrefs (overrides/home.html)
8. Four AVIF filenames (overrides/home.html + src/assets/hero/)
9. Brand colors (src/assets/pai-theme.css)
10. Logo files (src/assets/pai-logo-white.{png,svg})

Find them in code with `grep -rn "AGENT:" .`.

## Conventions

- No JavaScript in the parallax
- Images must be AVIF, named `N-name@4x.avif` (N = depth-sort order)
- Run `mkdocs build --strict` before pushing
- Don't commit `_site/`
- Use `{{ '...' | url }}` for internal links, not raw `href`

## Credits

Parallax CSS originates from [squidfunk/mkdocs-material](https://github.com/squidfunk/mkdocs-material) — MIT License, Martin Donath.
